title: BEM-924 curator response
objective:
Create file governance/audit/mailbox/from_curator/BEM924_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_RESPONSE.md with content # BEM-924 FINAL WORKING SYSTEM PROTOCOL RESPONSE
Status: APPROVED_WITH_REQUIREMENTS
Findings:
- Worker approves the final unified working-system protocol with requirements.
- Previous BEM-919..BEM-923 documents were useful but fragmented; BEM-924 must be the single implementation protocol.
- The protocol must answer: what exists now, what is not working, what must be built, how it is verified, and where operator gates are required.
- Telegram, provider model, prompts, registries, dispatch, managed channel, GitHub Actions, proof and Claude re-audit are modules of one system, not separate protocols.
- Claude requirements and operator corrections must be acceptance criteria.
Evidence:
- governance/runtime/curator_dispatch/BEM924_AGENT_CONTEXT_READ.md
- governance/audit/mailbox/to_curator/BEM924_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_REQUEST.md
- governance/audit/internal/results/BEM924_WORKER_FINAL_WORKING_SYSTEM_PROTOCOL_REVIEW.md
Required final roadmap:
- BEM-925: SSOT foundation: elements_registry, contours_registry, providers_registry, provider policies, prompt templates, object refs.
- BEM-926: Core workflow hardening: concurrency, release_proof_manifest, non-null CI/Git SHA, proof policy, fixed re-audit checklist.
- BEM-927: Dispatch and managed channel runtime: provider-aware queue lifecycle, auto-trigger decision, managed channel consumer, event log transitions.
- BEM-928: Object and contour execution lifecycle: curator/object runners, internal contour algorithm, provider-aware failover, role reports.
- BEM-929: Telegram operator interface: input envelope, mapping to managed channel, GD Curator primary route, optional curator routes, canonical reports, report_period_config owner OPERATOR.
- BEM-930: E2E operational tests: mock Telegram command -> GD Curator -> DIR -> WRK -> provider-aware contour -> proof -> canonical report; mock_status and production_status split.
- BEM-931: Production operator gate: secret names, webhook, schedule/daemon model, issue #31 permission, paid API/LLM runtime, real pilot choice.
- BEM-932: Claude re-audit package and release decision: bundle checklist, evidence, gaps, PASS/CONDITIONAL/FAIL.
Definition of working system:
- Operator task enters through approved interface.
- Envelope routes to GD Curator.
- Tasks move vertically through curators and horizontally between contours.
- Logical roles are executed by selected providers with failover evidence.
- Every transition is written to event log/managed channel/dispatch state.
- Every release PASS has proof and non-null CI/Git SHA.
- Operator receives canonical report on demand or configured period.
- Claude can re-audit evidence without relying on GPT self-claims.
Recommendation:
- Submit BEM-924 final protocol to operator for review.
No issue comments.
